message:
Integrate toolchain failure detection with graceful local fallback

This commit completes the toolchain failure integration by updating the
hook's compilation result handling to detect toolchain-related failures
and fall back to local execution gracefully.

Hook behavior changes (rch/src/hook.rs):
- Changed execute_remote_compilation return type from exit_code to a result
  struct containing both exit_code and stderr output
- Added toolchain failure detection check: is_toolchain_failure(&result.stderr, result.exit_code)
- On toolchain failure: logs warning and returns HookOutput::allow() for local fallback
- On other failures: continues to deny with error message (remote already tried)
- On success: continues to deny (artifacts transferred, no local re-execution needed)

This ensures that when a remote worker lacks the required Rust toolchain
(e.g., nightly-2024-01-15 not installed, rustup not available), the hook
gracefully falls back to local compilation rather than failing entirely.
The fail-open philosophy is maintained: toolchain issues on workers should
not block the agent's work.

E2E test expansion (scripts/e2e_test.sh):
- Added explicit toolchain specification test scenarios:
  - tc_nightly_install_fail: nightly-2024-01-15 install failure -> allow
  - tc_stable_no_rustup: stable without rustup -> allow
  - tc_beta_install_fail: beta-2024-02-01 install failure -> allow
  - tc_version_no_rustup: 1.75.0 without rustup -> allow
- Each scenario tests a specific toolchain format with appropriate failure mode
- Legacy tests preserved for backward compatibility

The combination of toolchain detection in rch-common, synchronization in
rch/toolchain.rs, verification in rch-wkr, and now fallback in hook.rs
creates a complete pipeline for handling toolchain requirements across
heterogeneous worker environments.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -437,10 +437,25 @@ run_e2e() {
         run_scenario "artifact_fail" "deny" "artifacts"
         run_scenario "remote_exit" "deny" "remote-exit"
 
-        # Toolchain synchronization scenarios
+        # Toolchain synchronization scenarios with explicit toolchain specification
         log "INFO" "E2E" "Running toolchain synchronization scenarios..."
+
+        # Test 1: Nightly toolchain with date - install failure should fall back
+        run_toolchain_scenario "tc_nightly_install_fail" "nightly-2024-01-15" "allow" "toolchain-install"
+
+        # Test 2: Stable toolchain - no rustup should fall back
+        run_toolchain_scenario "tc_stable_no_rustup" "stable" "allow" "no-rustup"
+
+        # Test 3: Beta with date - install failure should fall back
+        run_toolchain_scenario "tc_beta_install_fail" "beta-2024-02-01" "allow" "toolchain-install"
+
+        # Test 4: Specific version - no rustup should fall back
+        run_toolchain_scenario "tc_version_no_rustup" "1.75.0" "allow" "no-rustup"
+
+        # Legacy tests without explicit toolchain (backward compatibility)
         run_scenario "toolchain_install_fail" "allow" "toolchain-install"
         run_scenario "no_rustup" "allow" "no-rustup"
+
         log "INFO" "E2E" "Toolchain scenarios complete"
         return
     fi
